revision.comment: 新しいページ：劔神社（山口県防府市にある神社）。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 劔神社 |画像 = [[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[山口県]][[防府市]]高井 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 4|緯度秒 = 33.27 |経度度 = 131|経度分 = 33|経度秒 = 13.87 |ISO = JP-35 |祭神 = [[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |例祭 = [[9月27日]]-[[9月28日|28日]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Yamaguchi }} [[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居（後背に[[右田ヶ岳]]）}}]] '''劔神社'''（つるぎじんじゃ）は、[[山口県]][[防府市]]高井にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 祭神 == 現在の祭神は次の1柱境内説明板。。 * [[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]] == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建は不詳。社伝では、第14代[[仲哀天皇]]の筑紫行幸の際に、夷狄降伏を祈願して八握剣を[[神体]]として祀ったという{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。また、かつては劔川上流の勝坂に鎮座したともいう{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}（[[文禄]]4年（[[1595年]]）に現在地に再興か{{Sfn|劔神社（式内社）|1980}}）。 [[六国史|国史]]では、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]9年（[[867年]]）に[[周防国]]の「剣神」の[[神階]]を従五位上から正五位下に昇叙する旨が記載されている{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では、周防国[[佐波郡]]に「剣神社」と記載され、[[式内社]]に列している。 [[室町時代]]には[[大内氏]]から武勇の神として崇敬され、社領も多く持ったという{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。また、[[豊臣秀吉]]は九州下向の際に祈願し、帰途には唐太鼓を奉納したという{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。 [[江戸時代]]の『防長風土注進案』によれば、本社三間造茅葺、拝殿茅葺三間三間半であり、他に釣屋・御供所・宝蔵などの社殿があった{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。また勝坂鎮座時には本社五間四面、中殿三間半三間、幣殿五間四面、釣屋一間半二間のそり橋、拝殿七間二間半であり、社前には能舞台があったが、山火事で焼失したという{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。 [[明治維新]]後、[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に[[近代社格制度]]において準郷社に列し、明治8年（[[1875年]]）に[[郷社]]に昇格している{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912}}。 === 神階 === * [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]9年（[[867年]]）8月16日、従五位上から正五位下（『[[日本三代実録]]』） - 表記は「剣神」。 == 境内 == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|社殿 File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|本殿 File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|境内鳥居 == 主な祭事 == * 祈年祭（[[4月10日]]） * 五穀豊穣家内安全祈願祭（[[7月20日]]） * 例祭（[[9月27日]]-[[9月28日|28日]]） * 新嘗祭（[[12月5日]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 境内説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編|author=|year=1912|chapter=劔神社|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/160}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 下』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）160-161コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会編|author=恵良宏|year=1980|chapter=劔神社|title=式内社調査報告 第22巻|publisher=[[皇學館大学]]出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|劔神社（式内社）|1980}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=剣神社|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 36 山口県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つるきしんしや ほうふし}} [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:山口県の旧郷社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 劔神社 |画像 = [[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[山口県]][[防府市]]高井 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 4|緯度秒 = 33.27 |経度度 = 131|経度分 = 33|経度秒 = 13.87 |ISO = JP-35 |祭神 = [[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |例祭 = [[9月27日]]-[[9月28日|28日]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Yamaguchi }} [[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居（後背に[[右田ヶ岳]]）}}]] '''劔神社'''（つるぎじんじゃ）は、[[山口県]][[防府市]]高井にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 祭神 == 現在の祭神は次の1柱境内説明板。。 * [[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]] == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建は不詳。社伝では、第14代[[仲哀天皇]]の筑紫行幸の際に、夷狄降伏を祈願して八握剣を[[神体]]として祀ったという{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。また、かつては劔川上流の勝坂に鎮座したともいう{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}（[[文禄]]4年（[[1595年]]）に現在地に再興か{{Sfn|劔神社（式内社）|1980}}）。 [[六国史|国史]]では、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]9年（[[867年]]）に[[周防国]]の「剣神」の[[神階]]を従五位上から正五位下に昇叙する旨が記載されている{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では、周防国[[佐波郡]]に「剣神社」と記載され、[[式内社]]に列している。 [[室町時代]]には[[大内氏]]から武勇の神として崇敬され、社領も多く持ったという{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。また、[[豊臣秀吉]]は九州下向の際に祈願し、帰途には唐太鼓を奉納したという{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。 [[江戸時代]]の『防長風土注進案』によれば、本社三間造茅葺、拝殿茅葺三間三間半であり、他に釣屋・御供所・宝蔵などの社殿があった{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。また勝坂鎮座時には本社五間四面、中殿三間半三間、幣殿五間四面、釣屋一間半二間のそり橋、拝殿七間二間半であり、社前には能舞台があったが、山火事で焼失したという{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。 [[明治維新]]後、[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に[[近代社格制度]]において準郷社に列し、明治8年（[[1875年]]）に[[郷社]]に昇格している{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912}}。 [[ファイル:Tsurugijinja 2009 07.JPG|サムネイル|2009年の豪雨による被害]] [[2009年]]7月に起きた[[平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|豪雨災害]]の際は、[[土石流]]により被害を受けた。 === 神階 === * [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]9年（[[867年]]）8月16日、従五位上から正五位下（『[[日本三代実録]]』） - 表記は「剣神」。 == 境内 == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|社殿 File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|本殿 File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|境内鳥居 == 主な祭事 == * 祈年祭（[[4月10日]]） * 五穀豊穣家内安全祈願祭（[[7月20日]]） * 例祭（[[9月27日]]-[[9月28日|28日]]） * 新嘗祭（[[12月5日]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 境内説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編|author=|year=1912|chapter=劔神社|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/160}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 下』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）160-161コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会編|author=恵良宏|year=1980|chapter=劔神社|title=式内社調査報告 第22巻|publisher=[[皇學館大学]]出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|劔神社（式内社）|1980}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=剣神社|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 36 山口県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つるきしんしや ほうふし}} [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:山口県の旧郷社]]

表記は「剣神」。 == 境内 == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|社殿 File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|本殿 File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|境内鳥居 == 主な祭事 == * 祈年祭（[[4月10日]]） * 五穀豊穣家内安全祈願祭（[[7月20日]]） * 例祭（[[9月27日]]-[[9月28日|28日]]） * 新嘗祭（[[12月5日]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 境内説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編|author=|year=1912|chapter=劔神社|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/160}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 下』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）160-161コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会編|author=恵良宏|year=1980|chapter=劔神社|title=式内社調査報告 第22巻|publisher=[[皇學館大学]]出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|劔神社（式内社）|1980}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=剣神社|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 36 山口県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つるきしんしや ほうふし}} [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:山口県の旧郷社]]

拝殿 |所在地 = [[山口県]][[防府市]]高井 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 4|緯度秒 = 33.27 |経度度 = 131|経度分 = 33|経度秒 = 13.87 |ISO = JP-35 |祭神 = [[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |例祭 = [[9月27日]]-[[9月28日|28日]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Yamaguchi }} [[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居（後背に[[右田ヶ岳]]）}}]] '''劔神社'''（つるぎじんじゃ）は、[[山口県]][[防府市]]高井にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 魚の食べ方 == * [[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]] == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建は不詳。社伝では、第14代[[仲哀天皇]]の筑紫行幸の際に、夷狄降伏を祈願して八握剣を[[神体]]として祀ったという{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。また、かつては劔川上流の勝坂に鎮座したともいう{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}（[[文禄]]4年（[[1595年]]）に現在地に再興か{{Sfn|劔神社（式内社）|1980}}）。 [[六国史|国史]]では、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]9年（[[867年]]）に[[周防国]]の「剣神」の[[神階]]を従五位上から正五位下に昇叙する旨が記載されている{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では、周防国[[佐波郡 (山口県)|佐波郡]]に「剣神社」と記載され、[[式内社]]に列している。 [[室町時代]]には[[大内氏]]から武勇の神として崇敬され、社領も多く持ったという{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。また、[[豊臣秀吉]]は九州下向の際に祈願し、帰途には唐太鼓を奉納したという{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。 [[江戸時代]]の『防長風土注進案』によれば、本社三間造茅葺、拝殿茅葺三間三間半であり、他に釣屋・御供所・宝蔵などの社殿があった{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。また勝坂鎮座時には本社五間四面、中殿三間半三間、幣殿五間四面、釣屋一間半二間のそり橋、拝殿七間二間半であり、社前には能舞台があったが、山火事で焼失したという{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。 [[明治維新]]後、[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に[[近代社格制度]]において準郷社に列し、明治8年（[[1875年]]）に[[郷社]]に昇格している{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912}}。 [[ファイル:Tsurugijinja 2009 07.JPG|サムネイル|2009年の豪雨による被害]] [[2009年]]7月に起きた[[平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|豪雨災害]]の際は、[[土石流]]により被害を受けた。 === 神階 === * [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]9年（[[867年]]）8月16日、従五位上から正五位下（『[[日本三代実録]]』） - 表記は「剣神」。 == 境内 == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|社殿 File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|本殿 File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|境内鳥居 == 主な祭事 == * 祈年祭（[[4月10日]]） * 五穀豊穣家内安全祈願祭（[[7月20日]]） * 例祭（[[9月27日]]-[[9月28日|28日]]） * 新嘗祭（[[12月5日]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 境内説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編|author=|year=1912|chapter=劔神社|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/160}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 下』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）160-161コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会編|author=恵良宏|year=1980|chapter=劔神社|title=式内社調査報告 第22巻|publisher=[[皇學館大学]]出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|劔神社（式内社）|1980}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=剣神社|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 36 山口県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つるきしんしや ほうふし}} [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:山口県の旧郷社]]

revision.text: [[category:https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%98%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)

]][[ja:%E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%98%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] {{Shrine |name = Tsurugi Shrine |image = [[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |location = {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|4|33.2|N|131|33|13.8|E|region:JP-35}} |main deity = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} |rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社}} |founded = Unknown |annual festival = {{ill|September 27|ja|9月27日}}–{{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日}} |map = Japan Yamaguchi }} [[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii with {{ill|Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳}} in the background}}]] '''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hōfu|ja|防府市}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} == History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}}. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4). In {{ill|867|ja|貞観9年}}, it is recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}}. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of {{ill|Suō Province|ja|周防国}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and in the {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return. The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨}} due to landslides. == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} (December 5) == Gallery == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hōfu]] [[Category:History of Hōfu]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[ja:劔神社]]

]][[ja:%E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%98%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]][[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:bump 1-25]] {{Shrine |name = Tsurugi Shrine |image = [[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |location = {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|4|33.2|N|131|33|13.8|E|region:JP-35}} |main deity = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} |rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社}} |founded = Unknown |annual festival = {{ill|September 27|ja|9月27日}}–{{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日}} |map = Japan Yamaguchi }} [[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii with {{ill|Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳}} in the background}}]] '''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hōfu|ja|防府市}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} == History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}}. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4). In {{ill|867|ja|貞観9年}}, it is recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下}}. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of {{ill|Suō Province|ja|周防国}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and in the {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return. The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨}} due to landslides. == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} (December 5) == Gallery == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)}} [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Buildings and structures in Hōfu]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:History of Hōfu]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Shikinai shrines]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[ja:劔神社]]

The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨}} due to landslides. == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} (December 5) == Gallery == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)}} [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Buildings and structures in Hōfu]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:History of Hōfu]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:劔神社]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |location = {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|4|33.2|N|131|33|13.8|E|region:JP-35}} |main deity = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} |rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社}} |founded = Unknown |annual festival = {{ill|September 27|ja|9月27日}}–{{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日}} |map = Japan Yamaguchi }} [[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii with {{ill|Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳}} in the background}}]] '''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hōfu|ja|防府市}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} == History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}}. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4). In {{ill|867|ja|貞観9年}}, it is recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of {{ill|Suō Province|ja|周防国}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and in the {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}} prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return. The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨}} due to landslides. == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} (December 5) == Gallery == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)}} [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Buildings and structures in Hōfu]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:History of Hōfu]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:oldest 100 pages as of Feb 11]][[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:劔神社]]

]][[ja:%E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%98%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)}}[[Category:11]] {{Shrine |name = Tsurugi Shrine |image = [[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |location = {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|4|33.2|N|131|33|13.8|E|region:JP-35}} |main deity = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} |rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (small), Former {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社}} |founded = Unknown |annual festival = {{ill|September 27|ja|9月27日}}–{{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日}} |map = Japan Yamaguchi }} [[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii with {{ill|Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳}} in the background}}]] '''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hōfu|ja|防府市}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社}} under the old shrine ranking system. == Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊}} == History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}}. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4). In {{ill|867|ja|貞観9年}}, it is recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of {{ill|Suō Province|ja|周防国}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}, and in the {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return. The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21st year 7月中国・九州北部豪雨}} due to landslides. == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} (December 5) == Gallery == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)}} [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hōfu]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Hōfu]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:11]][[en:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:劔神社]]

]][[ja:%E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%98%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)}}[[Category:11]] {{Shrine |name = Tsurugi Shrine |image = [[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg|250px]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 劔神社 |画像 = [[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地 = [[山口県]][[防府市]]高井1154-1 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 4|緯度秒 = 33.2 |経度度 = 131|経度分 = 33|経度秒 = 13.8 |ISO = JP-35 |祭神 = [[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |例祭 = [[9月27日]]-[[9月28日|28日]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Yamaguchi }} [[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居（後背に[[右田ヶ岳]]）}}]] '''劔神社'''（つるぎじんじゃ）は、[[山口県]][[防府市]]高井にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 祭神 == 現在の祭神は次の1柱境内説明板。。 * [[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]] == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建は不詳。社伝では、第14代[[仲哀天皇]]の筑紫行幸の際に、夷狄降伏を祈願して八握剣を[[神体]]として祀ったという{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。また、かつては劔川上流の勝坂に鎮座したともいう{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}（[[文禄]]4年（[[1595年]]）に現在地に再興か{{Sfn|劔神社（式内社）|1980}}）。 [[六国史|国史]]では、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]9年（[[867年]]）に[[周防国]]の「剣神」の[[神階]]を従五位上から正五位下に昇叙する旨が記載されている{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では、周防国[[佐波郡 (山口県)|佐波郡]]に「剣神社」と記載され、[[式内社]]に列している。 [[室町時代]]には[[大内氏]]から武勇の神として崇敬され、社領も多く持ったという{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。また、[[豊臣秀吉]]は九州下向の際に祈願し、帰途には唐太鼓を奉納したという{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。 [[江戸時代]]の『防長風土注進案』によれば、本社三間造茅葺、拝殿茅葺三間三間半であり、他に釣屋・御供所・宝蔵などの社殿があった{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。また勝坂鎮座時には本社五間四面、中殿三間半三間、幣殿五間四面、釣屋一間半二間のそり橋、拝殿七間二間半であり、社前には能舞台があったが、山火事で焼失したという{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。 [[明治維新]]後、[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に[[近代社格制度]]において準郷社に列し、明治8年（[[1875年]]）に[[郷社]]に昇格している{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912}}。 [[ファイル:Tsurugijinja 2009 07.JPG|サムネイル|2009年の豪雨による被害]] [[2009年]]7月に起きた[[平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|豪雨災害]]の際は、[[土石流]]により被害を受けた。 === 神階 === * [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]9年（[[867年]]）8月16日、従五位上から正五位下（『[[日本三代実録]]』） - 表記は「剣神」。 == 境内 == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|社殿 File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|本殿 File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|境内鳥居 == 主な祭事 == * 祈年祭（[[4月10日]]） * 五穀豊穣家内安全祈願祭（[[7月20日]]） * 例祭（[[9月27日]]-[[9月28日|28日]]） * 新嘗祭（[[12月5日]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 境内説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編|author=|year=1912|chapter=劔神社|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/160}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 下』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）160-161コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会編|author=恵良宏|year=1980|chapter=劔神社|title=式内社調査報告 第22巻|publisher=[[皇學館大学]]出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|劔神社（式内社）|1980}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=剣神社|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 36 山口県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つるきしんしや ほうふし}} [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:山口県の旧郷社]]

拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地 = [[山口県]][[防府市]]高井1154-1 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 4|緯度秒 = 33.2 |経度度 = 131|経度分 = 33|経度秒 = 13.8 |ISO = JP-35 |祭神 = [[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |例祭 = [[9月27日]]-[[9月28日|28日]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Yamaguchi }} [[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居（後背に[[右田ヶ岳]]）}}]] '''劔神社'''（つるぎじんじゃ）は、[[山口県]][[防府市]]高井にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 祭神 == 現在の祭神は次の1柱境内説明板。。 * [[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]] == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建は不詳。社伝では、第14代[[仲哀天皇]]の筑紫行幸の際に、夷狄降伏を祈願して八握剣を[[神体]]として祀ったという{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。また、かつては劔川上流の勝坂に鎮座したともいう{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}（[[文禄]]4年（[[1595年]]）に現在地に再興か{{Sfn|劔神社（式内社）|1980}}）。 [[六国史|国史]]では、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]9年（[[867年]]）に[[周防国]]の「剣神」の[[神階]]を従五位上から正五位下に昇叙する旨が記載されている{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では、周防国[[佐波郡 (山口県)|佐波郡]]に「剣神社」と記載され、[[式内社]]に列している。 [[室町時代]]には[[大内氏]]から武勇の神として崇敬され、社領も多く持ったという{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。また、[[豊臣秀吉]]は九州下向の際に祈願し、帰途には唐太鼓を奉納したという{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。 [[江戸時代]]の『防長風土注進案』によれば、本社三間造茅葺、拝殿茅葺三間三間半であり、他に釣屋・御供所・宝蔵などの社殿があった{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。また勝坂鎮座時には本社五間四面、中殿三間半三間、幣殿五間四面、釣屋一間半二間のそり橋、拝殿七間二間半であり、社前には能舞台があったが、山火事で焼失したという{{Sfn|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}。 [[明治維新]]後、[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に[[近代社格制度]]において準郷社に列し、明治8年（[[1875年]]）に[[郷社]]に昇格している{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912}}。 [[ファイル:Tsurugijinja 2009 07.JPG|サムネイル|2009年の豪雨による被害]] [[2009年]]7月に起きた[[平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|豪雨災害]]の際は、[[土石流]]により被害を受けた。 === 神階 === * [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]9年（[[867年]]）8月16日、従五位上から正五位下（『[[日本三代実録]]』） - 表記は「剣神」。 == 境内 == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|社殿 File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|本殿 File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|境内鳥居 == 主な祭事 == * 祈年祭（[[4月10日]]） * 五穀豊穣家内安全祈願祭（[[7月20日]]） * 例祭（[[9月27日]]-[[9月28日|28日]]） * 新嘗祭（[[12月5日]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 境内説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編|author=|year=1912|chapter=劔神社|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/160}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 下』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）160-161コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会編|author=恵良宏|year=1980|chapter=劔神社|title=式内社調査報告 第22巻|publisher=[[皇學館大学]]出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|劔神社（式内社）|1980}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1980|chapter=剣神社|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 36 山口県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|剣神社（平凡社）|1980}}}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つるきしんしや ほうふし}} [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:周防国の式内社]] [[Category:山口県の旧郷社]]

revision.text:
]] {{Shrine |name = Tsurugi Shrine |image = [[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |location = {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|4|33.2|N|131|33|13.8|E|region:JP-35}} |main deity = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (small), Former {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |founded = Unknown |annual festival = {{ill|September 27|ja|9月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}–{{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |map = Japan Yamaguchi }} [[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii with {{ill|Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the background}}]] '''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Hōfu|ja|防府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the old shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4). In {{ill|867|ja|貞観9th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of {{ill|Suō Province|ja|周防国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in the {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return. The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|qq=draft|qq=draft}} due to landslides. == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (December 5) == Gallery == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|劔神社 (防府市)|version=102167194|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hōfu]] [[Category:History of Hōfu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] }} [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[ja:%E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%98%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[ja:劔神社]]

]] {{Shrine |name = Tsurugi Shrine |image = [[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |location = {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|4|33.2|N|131|33|13.8|E|region:JP-35}} |main deity = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (small), Former {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |founded = Unknown |annual festival = {{ill|September 27|ja|9月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}–{{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |map = Japan Yamaguchi }} [[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii with {{ill|Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the background}}]] '''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Hōfu|ja|防府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the old shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4). In {{ill|867|ja|貞観9th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of {{ill|Suō Province|ja|周防国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in the {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return. The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|qq=draft|qq=draft}} due to landslides. == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (December 5) == Gallery == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:translated pages without meta categories]] {{translated page|ja|劔神社 (防府市)|version=102167194|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hōfu]] [[Category:History of Hōfu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] }} [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[ja:%E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%98%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[ja:劔神社]]

The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|qq=draft|qq=draft}} due to landslides. == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (December 5) == Gallery == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|劔神社 (防府市)|version=102167194|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hōfu]] [[Category:History of Hōfu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] }} [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[ja:%E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%98%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[ja:劔神社]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |location = {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|4|33.2|N|131|33|13.8|E|region:JP-35}} |main deity = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (small), Former {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |founded = Unknown |annual festival = {{ill|September 27|ja|9月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}–{{ill|September 28|ja|9月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |map = Japan Yamaguchi }} [[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii with {{ill|Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the background}}]] '''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Hōfu|ja|防府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the old shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4). In {{ill|867|ja|貞観9th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of {{ill|Suō Province|ja|周防国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in the {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return. The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|qq=draft|qq=draft}} due to landslides. == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (December 5) == Gallery == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|劔神社 (防府市)|version=102167194|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hōfu]] [[Category:History of Hōfu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] }} [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[ja:%E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%98%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[ja:劔神社]]

The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of {{ill|Suō Province|ja|周防国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in the {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the old shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4). In {{ill|867|ja|貞観9th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in the {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return. The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|qq=draft|qq=draft}} due to landslides. == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (December 5) == Gallery == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|劔神社 (防府市)|version=102167194|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hōfu]] [[Category:History of Hōfu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[ja:%E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%98%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[ja:劔神社]]

The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|qq=}} due to landslides. == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}} (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiname-sai|qq=}} (December 5) == Gallery == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|劔神社 (防府市)|version=102167194|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hōfu]] [[Category:History of Hōfu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[ja:%E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%98%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[ja:劔神社]]

Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|qq=}} in the background}}]] '''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Hōfu|ja|防府市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfu|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|qq=}} under the old shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūai|qq=}} (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shintai|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintai|qq=}}. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4). In {{ill|867|ja|貞観9th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/867|qq=}}, it is recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}} that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of {{ill|Suō Province|ja|周防国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Province|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōuchi clan|qq=}}, and in the {{ill|Azuchi-Momoyama period|ja|安土桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi-Momoyama period|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}} prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return. The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|qq=}} due to landslides. == Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}} (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiname-sai|qq=}} (December 5) == Gallery == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|劔神社 (防府市)|version=102167194|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hōfu]] [[Category:History of Hōfu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[ja:%E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%98%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[ja:劔神社]]

It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4). In {{ill|867|ja|貞観9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/867|qq=}}, it is recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]]. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and in the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|qq=}} in the background}}]] '''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|qq=}} under the old shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]] (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4). In {{ill|867|ja|貞観9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/867|qq=}}, it is recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]]. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and in the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return. The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|qq=}} due to landslides. == Annual Festivals == * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (December 5) == Gallery == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|劔神社 (防府市)|version=102167194|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hōfu]] [[Category:History of Hōfu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[ja:%E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%98%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[ja:劔神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suō Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]]

Mitake (Yamaguchi)|qq=}} in the background}}]] '''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|qq=}} under the old shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]] (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4). In {{ill|867|ja|貞観9年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/867|qq=}}, it is recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]]. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and in the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return. The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|qq=}} due to landslides. == Annual Festivals == * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (December 5) == Gallery == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|劔神社 (防府市)|version=102167194|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hōfu]] [[Category:History of Hōfu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[ja:%E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%98%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:劔神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suō Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the old shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} == History == The shrine's origins are unclear. The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|qq=}} due to landslides. == Annual Festivals == * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (December 5) == Gallery == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|劔神社 (防府市)|version=102167194|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[ja:%E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%98%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:劔神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suō Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] {{Commons category|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} [[ja:劔神社 (防府市)]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |location = [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|4|33.2|N|131|33|13.8|E|region:JP-35}} |main deity = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (small), Former {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |founded = Unknown |annual festival = [[:en:September 27|September 27]]–[[:en:September 28|September 28]] |map = Japan Yamaguchi }} [[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii with {{ill|Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|qq=}} in the background}}]] '''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the old shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} == History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]] (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4). In [[:en:867|867]], it is recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]]. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and in the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return. The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|qq=}} due to landslides. == Annual Festivals == * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (December 5) == Gallery == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|劔神社 (防府市)|version=102167194|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[ja:%E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%98%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:劔神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suō Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] {{Commons category|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} [[ja:劔神社 (防府市)]]

The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|qq=}} due to landslides. == Annual Festivals == * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (December 5) == Gallery == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|劔神社 (防府市)|version=102167194|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[ja:%E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%98%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:劔神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suō Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Hōfu City]] [[Category:防府の Municipal History]] {{Commons category|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} [[ja:劔神社 (防府市)]]

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |location = [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|4|33.2|N|131|33|13.8|E|region:JP-35}} |main deity = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), Former {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |founded = Unknown |annual festival = [[:en:September 27|September 27]]–[[:en:September 28|September 28]] |map = Japan Yamaguchi }} [[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii with {{ill|Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|qq=}} in the background}}]] '''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the old shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} == History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]] (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4). In [[:en:867|867]], it is recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]]. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and in the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return. The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|qq=}} due to landslides. == Annual Festivals == * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (December 5) == Gallery == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|劔神社 (防府市)|version=102167194|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[ja:%E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%98%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:劔神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suō Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Hōfu City]] [[Category:防府の Municipal History]] {{Commons category|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} [[ja:劔神社 (防府市)]]

The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]].
