The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|qq=}} due to landslides. == Annual Festivals == * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (December 5) == Gallery == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|劔神社 (防府市)|version=102167194|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[ja:%E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%98%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:劔神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suō Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Hōfu City]] [[Category:History of Hofu City]] {{Commons category|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} [[ja:劔神社 (防府市)]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|qq=}} in the background}}]] '''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the old shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} == History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]] (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4). In [[:en:867|867]], it is recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]]. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and in the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return. The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|qq=}} due to landslides. == Annual Festivals == * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (December 5) == Gallery == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|劔神社 (防府市)|version=102167194|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[sn:E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%98%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:劔神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suō Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Hōfu City]] [[Category:History of Hofu City]] {{Commons category|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} [[ja:劔神社 (防府市)]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

In [[:en:867|867]], it is recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]]. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and in the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return. The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|qq=}} due to landslides. == Annual Festivals == * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (December 5) == Gallery == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|劔神社 (防府市)|version=102167194|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[sn:E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%98%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:劔神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suō Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Hōfu City]] [[Category:History of Hofu City]] {{Commons category|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} [[ja:劔神社 (防府市)]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |location = [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{coord|34|4|33.2|N|131|33|13.8|E|region:JP-35}} |main deity = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), Former {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |founded = Unknown |annual festival = [[:en:September 27|September 27]]–[[:en:September 28|September 28]] |map = Japan Yamaguchi }} [[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii with {{ill|Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|qq=}} in the background}}]] '''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the old shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} == History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]] (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4). In [[:en:867|867]], it is recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]]. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and in the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return. The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|qq=}} due to landslides. == Annual Festivals == * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (December 5) == Gallery == File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|劔神社 (防府市)|version=102167194|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] }} [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Hōfu)]] [[sn:E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E9%98%B2%E5%BA%9C%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:劔神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suō Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Hōfu City]] [[Category:History of Hofu City]] {{Commons category|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}} [[ja:劔神社 (防府市)]]

'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system.

== Enshrined deity ==
* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}

== History ==
The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]] (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4).

In [[:en:867|867]], it is recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}.

The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]].

During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and in the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return.

The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|qq=|1=July 2009 Chugoku-Northern Kyushu Heavy Rainfall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10845135}} due to landslides.

== Annual Festivals ==
* [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (April 10)
* Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20)
* Main Annual Festival (September 27–28)
* [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (December 5)

[[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii with {{ill|Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|1=Mount Migita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412165}} in the background}}]]

'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system.

== Enshrined deity ==
* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}

== History ==
The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]] (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4).

In [[:en:867|867]], it is recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}.

The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]].

During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and in the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return.

The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|1=July 2009 Chugoku-Northern Kyushu Heavy Rainfall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10845135}} due to landslides.

== Annual Festivals ==
* [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (April 10)
* Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20)
* Main Annual Festival (September 27–28)
* [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (December 5)

[[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii with {{ill|Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|1=Mount Migita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412165}} in the background}}]]

'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system.

== Enshrined deity ==
* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}

== History ==
The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]] (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4).

In [[:en:867|867]], it is recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}.

The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]].

During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and in the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return.

The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|1=July 2009 Chugoku-Northern Kyushu Heavy Rainfall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10845135}} due to landslides.

== Annual Festivals ==
* [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (April 10)
* Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20)
* Main Annual Festival (September 27–28)
* [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (December 5)

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]] (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4).
In [[:en:867|867]], it is recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}.
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]].
During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and in the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return.
The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|1=July 2009 Chugoku-Northern Kyushu Heavy Rainfall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10845135}} due to landslides.

== History ==
The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]] (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4).

In [[:en:867|867]], it is recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}.

The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]].

[[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii with {{ill|Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|1=Mount Migita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412165}} in the background}}]]

'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system.

== Enshrined deity ==
* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}

== History ==
The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]] (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4).

In [[:en:867|867]], it is recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}.

The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]].

During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and in the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return.

The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|1=July 2009 Chugoku-Northern Kyushu Heavy Rainfall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10845135}} due to landslides.

== Annual Festivals ==
* [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (April 10)
* Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20)
* Main Annual Festival (September 27–28)
* [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (December 5)

[[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii with {{ill|Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|1=Mount Migita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412165}} in the background}}]]

'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system.

== Enshrined deity ==
* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}

== History ==
The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]] (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4).

In [[:en:867|867]], it is recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}.

The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]].

During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and in the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return.

The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|1=July 2009 Chugoku-Northern Kyushu Heavy Rainfall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10845135}} due to landslides.

== Annual Festivals ==
* [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (April 10)
* Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20)
* Main Annual Festival (September 27–28)
* [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (December 5)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:劔神社 (防府市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11398386]] Wikidata name: Tsurugi Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:32, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} ]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tsurugi Shrine |image = File:Tsurugijinja-haiden01.jpg |location = [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|35.957839|N|136.055394|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |main deity = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), Former {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |founded = Unknown |annual festival = [[:en:September 27|September 27]]–[[:en:September 28|September 28]] |map = Japan Yamaguchi |location = {{ill|Echizen|WD=Q1280737}} }}
[[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii with {{ill|Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|1=Mount Migita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412165}} in the background}}]]
'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]] (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4).
In [[:en:867|867]], it is recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}.
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]].
During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and in the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return.
The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|1=July 2009 Chugoku-Northern Kyushu Heavy Rainfall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10845135}} due to landslides.
== Annual Festivals == * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (December 5)
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:劔神社 (防府市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11398386]] Wikidata name: Tsurugi Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:32, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} ]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tsurugi Shrine |image = File:Tsurugijinja-haiden01.jpg |location = [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|35.957839|N|136.055394|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |main deity = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), Former {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |founded = Unknown |annual festival = [[:en:September 27|September 27]]–[[:en:September 28|September 28]] |map = Japan Yamaguchi |location = {{ill|Echizen|WD=Q1280737}} }}
[[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii with {{ill|Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|1=Mount Migita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412165}} in the background}}]]
'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]] (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4).
In [[:en:867|867]], it is recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}.
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]].
During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and in the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return.
The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|1=July 2009 Chugoku-Northern Kyushu Heavy Rainfall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10845135}} due to landslides.
== Annual Festivals == * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (December 5)
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:劔神社 (防府市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:劔神社 (防府市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:劔神社 (防府市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11398386]] Wikidata name: Tsurugi Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:32, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} ]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tsurugi Shrine |image = File:Tsurugijinja-haiden01.jpg |location = [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|35.957839|N|136.055394|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |main deity = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), Former {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |founded = Unknown |annual festival = [[:en:September 27|September 27]]–[[:en:September 28|September 28]] |map = Japan Yamaguchi |location = {{ill|Echizen|WD=Q1280737}} }}
[[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii with {{ill|Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|1=Mount Migita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412165}} in the background}}]]
'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]] (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4).
In [[:en:867|867]], it is recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}.
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]].
During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and in the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return.
The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|1=July 2009 Chugoku-Northern Kyushu Heavy Rainfall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10845135}} due to landslides.
== Annual Festivals == * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (December 5)
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:劔神社 (防府市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:劔神社 (防府市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:劔神社 (防府市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398386]] Wikidata name: Tsurugi Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:32, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} ]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tsurugi Shrine |image = File:Tsurugijinja-haiden01.jpg |location = [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|35.957839|N|136.055394|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |main deity = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), Former {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |founded = Unknown |annual festival = [[:en:September 27|September 27]]–[[:en:September 28|September 28]] |map = Japan Yamaguchi |location = {{ill|Echizen|WD=Q1280737}} }}
[[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii with {{ill|Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|1=Mount Migita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412165}} in the background}}]]
'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]] (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4).
In [[:en:867|867]], it is recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}.
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]].
During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and in the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return.
The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|1=July 2009 Chugoku-Northern Kyushu Heavy Rainfall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10845135}} due to landslides.
== Annual Festivals == * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (December 5)
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds </gallery>

[[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii with {{ill|Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|1=Mount Migita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412165}} in the background}}]]
'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]] (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4).
In [[:en:867|867]], it is recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}.
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]].
During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and in the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return.
The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|1=July 2009 Chugoku-Northern Kyushu Heavy Rainfall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10845135}} due to landslides.
== Annual Festivals == * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (December 5)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:劔神社 (防府市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:劔神社 (防府市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:劔神社 (防府市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398386]] Wikidata name: Tsurugi Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:32, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} ]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tsurugi Shrine |image = File:Tsurugijinja-haiden01.jpg |location = [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu, Yamaguchi]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|35.957839|N|136.055394|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |main deity = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), Former {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |founded = Unknown |annual festival = [[:en:September 27|September 27]]–[[:en:September 28|September 28]] |map = Japan Yamaguchi |location = {{ill|Echizen|WD=Q1280737}} }}
[[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii with {{ill|Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|1=Mount Migita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412165}} in the background}}]]
'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]] (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]]. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4).
In [[:en:867|867]], it is recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}.
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]].
During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]], and in the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]], [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return.
The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|1=July 2009 Chugoku-Northern Kyushu Heavy Rainfall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10845135}} due to landslides.
== Annual Festivals == * [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (December 5)
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds </gallery>

In {{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867}}, it is recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}.
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of {{ill|Suō Province|en|Suō Province|WD=Q1053037|lt=Suō Province}}.
During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}, the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|en|Ōuchi clan|WD=Q1348909|lt=Ōuchi clan}}, and in the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi-Momoyama period}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return.
The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|1=July 2009 Chugoku-Northern Kyushu Heavy Rainfall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10845135}} due to landslides.
== Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai}} (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai}} (December 5)

[[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii with {{ill|Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|1=Mount Migita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412165}} in the background}}]]
'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hōfu|en|Hōfu|WD=Q502209|lt=Hōfu}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Chūai}} (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the {{ill|Shintai|en|Shintai|WD=Q327532|lt=Shintai}}. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4).
In {{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867}}, it is recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}.
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of {{ill|Suō Province|en|Suō Province|WD=Q1053037|lt=Suō Province}}.
During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}, the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|en|Ōuchi clan|WD=Q1348909|lt=Ōuchi clan}}, and in the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi-Momoyama period}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return.
The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|1=July 2009 Chugoku-Northern Kyushu Heavy Rainfall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10845135}} due to landslides.
== Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai}} (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai}} (December 5)
