<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:劔神社 (防府市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:劔神社 (防府市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:劔神社 (防府市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398386]] Wikidata name: Tsurugi Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:32, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} ]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tsurugi Shrine |image = File:Tsurugijinja-haiden01.jpg |location = {{ill|Hōfu|en|Hōfu|WD=Q502209|lt=Hōfu, Yamaguchi}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|35.957839|N|136.055394|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |main deity = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), Former {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |founded = Unknown |annual festival = {{ill|September 27|en|September 27|WD=Q2884|lt=September 27}}–{{ill|September 28|en|September 28|WD=Q2883|lt=September 28}} |map = Japan Yamaguchi |location = {{ill|Echizen|WD=Q1280737}} }}
[[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii with {{ill|Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|1=Mount Migita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412165}} in the background}}]]
'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hōfu|en|Hōfu|WD=Q502209|lt=Hōfu}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Chūai}} (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the {{ill|Shintai|en|Shintai|WD=Q327532|lt=Shintai}}. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4).
In {{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867}}, it is recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}.
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of {{ill|Suō Province|en|Suō Province|WD=Q1053037|lt=Suō Province}}.
During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}, the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|en|Ōuchi clan|WD=Q1348909|lt=Ōuchi clan}}, and in the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi-Momoyama period}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return.
The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|1=July 2009 Chugoku-Northern Kyushu Heavy Rainfall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10845135}} due to landslides.
== Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai}} (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai}} (December 5)
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), keidai-torii.jpg|Torii within the shrine grounds </gallery>

During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}, the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|en|Ōuchi clan|WD=Q1348909|lt=Ōuchi clan}}, and in the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi-Momoyama period}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return.
The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|1=July 2009 Chugoku-Northern Kyushu Heavy Rainfall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10845135}} due to landslides.
== Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai}} (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai}} (December 5)

'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hōfu|en|Hōfu|WD=Q502209|lt=Hōfu}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Chūai}} (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the {{ill|Shintai|en|Shintai|WD=Q327532|lt=Shintai}}. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4).
In {{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867}}, it is recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}.
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of {{ill|Suō Province|en|Suō Province|WD=Q1053037|lt=Suō Province}}.
During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}, the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|en|Ōuchi clan|WD=Q1348909|lt=Ōuchi clan}}, and in the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi-Momoyama period}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return.
The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|1=July 2009 Chugoku-Northern Kyushu Heavy Rainfall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10845135}} due to landslides.

[[File:Tsurugi-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii with {{ill|Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|ja|右田ヶ岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Mitake (Yamaguchi)|1=Mount Migita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412165}} in the background}}]]
'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hōfu|en|Hōfu|WD=Q502209|lt=Hōfu|1=Hōfu}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}} (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the {{ill|Shintai|en|Shintai|WD=Q327532|lt=Shintai|1=Shintai}}. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4).
In {{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867|1=867}}, it is recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}.
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of {{ill|Suō Province|en|Suō Province|WD=Q1053037|lt=Suō Province|1=Suō Province}}.
During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|en|Ōuchi clan|WD=Q1348909|lt=Ōuchi clan|1=Ōuchi clan}}, and in the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi-Momoyama period|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return.
The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|1=July 2009 Chugoku-Northern Kyushu Heavy Rainfall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10845135}} due to landslides.
== Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}} (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} (December 5)

It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4).

The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of {{ill|Suō Province|en|Suō Province|WD=Q1053037|lt=Suō Province|1=Suō Province}}.
During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|en|Ōuchi clan|WD=Q1348909|lt=Ōuchi clan|1=Ōuchi clan}}, and in the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi-Momoyama period|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return.
The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|1=July 2009 Chugoku-Northern Kyushu Heavy Rainfall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10845135}} due to landslides.
== Annual Festivals == * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}} (April 10) * Prayer Festival for Bountiful Harvest & Family Safety (July 20) * Main Annual Festival (September 27–28) * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} (December 5)

'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hōfu|en|Hōfu|WD=Q502209|lt=Hōfu|1=Hōfu}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha (shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined deity == * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
== History == The shrine's origins are unclear. According to legend, it was founded during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}} (14th Emperor of Japan) when he prayed for victory over foreign invaders, enshrining a sacred sword (八握剣) as the {{ill|Shintai|en|Shintai|WD=Q327532|lt=Shintai|1=Shintai}}. It was originally located upstream along the Tsurugi River at a site called Katsuzaka and was relocated to its current location around 1595 (Bunroku 4).
In {{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867|1=867}}, it is recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} that the shrine, referred to as "Kenjin" (剣神), was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}.
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} compiled in 927, as "Tsurugi Shrine" located in Saba District of {{ill|Suō Province|en|Suō Province|WD=Q1053037|lt=Suō Province|1=Suō Province}}.
During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, the shrine was revered as a god of martial prowess by the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|en|Ōuchi clan|WD=Q1348909|lt=Ōuchi clan|1=Ōuchi clan}}, and in the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi-Momoyama period|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} prayed here during his Kyushu campaign, offering a drum upon his victorious return.
The shrine sustained damage during the {{ill|July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|ja|平成21年7月中国・九州北部豪雨|zh|2009年日本西部暴雨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 2009 Chūgoku and Northern Kyushu heavy rain|1=July 2009 Chugoku-Northern Kyushu Heavy Rainfall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10845135}} due to landslides.
